note: News is categorized for StallPlus messaging. Hidden camera and safety stories should be framed as additional privacy protection, not guaranteed prevention.

categoryCounts.label: Viral Social Media About US Bathroom Stalls

articles.title: Heath duo accused of using public bathroom camera to make child porn
articles.description: Columbus man who ran a red light and T-boned an SUV at Sunbury and Morse was later fatally struck; police probe. * A Heath man and woman face charges after police allege they made child pornography. * Police accuse the pair of placing a hidden camera inside a public bathroom in Newark. * Authorities discovered the videos on a laptop and other devices on June 24. A Heath man and woman face criminal charges after prosecutors allege in court documents they were creating child pornography when they put a camera inside a public bathroom in Newark. Brianna N. Nethers, 23, and John M. Nethers, 43, each face one count of pandering sexual material involving a minor, Licking County Common Pleas Court records show. In a criminal complaint, police allege Brianna Nethers put a hidden camera from her purse inside a public bathroom in Newark before bringing a minor to that area to engage in sexual activity. Police allege that John Nethers then uploaded the video to his laptop. Police allege another video shows Brianna Nethers putting the camera on a hidden purse in a public restroom in Licking County, though the complaint doesn't state in which municipality. In the video, several people use the bathroom, including minors, police allege in the complaint Newark police found several devices June 24, including John Nethers' laptop on which they allege they found files containing the videos, according to the complaint. Deputies arrested both of them June 24, and they each had bond hearings June 25, court records show.
articles.stallplusAngle: Hidden camera stories raise restroom privacy expectations. "Heath duo accused of using public bathroom camera to make child porn" supports additional sightline protection messaging without claiming surveillance detection or guaranteed prevention.

articles.title: Heath duo accused of using public bathroom camera to make child porn
articles.description: A Heath man and woman face criminal charges after prosecutors allege in court documents they were creating child pornography when they put a camera inside a public bathroom in Newark. Brianna N. Nethers, 23, and John M. Nethers, 43, each face one count of pandering sexual material involving a minor, Licking County Common Pleas Court records show. In a criminal complaint, police allege Brianna Nethers put a hidden camera from her purse inside a public bathroom in Newark before bringing a minor to that area to engage in sexual activity. Police allege that John Nethers then uploaded the video to his laptop. Police allege another video shows Brianna Nethers putting the camera on a hidden purse in a public restroom in Licking County, though the complaint doesn't state in which municipality. In the video, several people use the bathroom, including minors, police allege in the complaint Newark police found several devices June 24, including John Nethers' laptop on which they allege they found files containing the videos, according to the complaint. Deputies arrested both of them June 24, and they each had bond hearings June 25, court records show. Advocate reporter Josué Perez can be reached at jhperez@newarkadvocate.com. This article originally appeared on Newark Advocate: Heath duo accused of using public bathroom camera to make child porn
articles.stallplusAngle: Hidden camera stories raise restroom privacy expectations. "Heath duo accused of using public bathroom camera to make child porn" supports additional sightline protection messaging without claiming surveillance detection or guaranteed prevention.

pendingReview.title: Violent carer, rapists and fraudsters: 26 criminals locked up in
pendingReview.description: A thug racially abused a lone woman in the street at night, threatened to "shank" her and then punched her in the face in a bid to rob her of money. Joe Wilson targeted his victim - a stranger - as she walked along Military Road in Canterbury in the early hours of January 5 last year. After initially running past her, he approached her from behind less than a minute later, stopped directly in front of her and stood very close to her face. Intoxicated Wilson then unleashed a torrent of racial abuse and demanded her wallet before striking her hard with his fist. He then ran off, leaving his victim petrified and in pain, Canterbury Crown Court was told, and she later told police how her attacker had swung his arm back and punched her "like she was a full-grown man". After a manhunt was launched, Wilson, of Herne Bay Road, Sturry, handed himself in to police the following month and, despite initially lying about being the assailant, he then confessed and subsequently pleaded guilty to the charge of attempted robbery. At his sentencing hearing, the court heard it was shortly before 2am that the woman encountered Wilson when he called her "a black ****" and asked, "What are you doing in this country? Give me your wallet," and he also threatened, "I'm going to shank you." The court also heard that the then 20-year-old also placed his hand in his waistband as if to indicate he had a knife. He also punched her with a clenched fist to her left cheek and earlier this month, the now 21-year-old was jailed for two years and five months for the crime. Warren Conn The discovery of a 25-minute video on an old mobile phone landed a rapist 14 years behind bars. Warren Conn was arrested in January after a woman found the clip of him sexually assaulting her several years earlier when he lived in Sandwich. She had no memory of the recording being made and never consented to the sexual activity depicted, and following a digital forensic investigation, further videos were uncovered indicating Conn had raped her. He was arrested by the Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI) at his home address in County Armagh, where he returned after living in Kent. Searches of his home uncovered two mobile phones and a laptop and these were seized and handed to Kent Police's digital forensics unit. A probe into the devices found a large quantity of indecent images of children. PSNI officers handed Conn over to Kent Police in Belfast, and he was flown back to Kent for the investigation to be continued. The 50-year-old was remanded in custody and was later charged with 15 sexual offences, including rape and possession of indecent images of children. After three interviews across two days, he admitted the offences and entered guilty pleas to all charges. Earlier this month, Conn, formerly of Kings Park Mews, Lurgan, was sentenced at Canterbury Crown Court to 14 years' imprisonment. Christopher Robinson A wife-beater subjected a mum-of-three to a campaign of horrendous abuse behind closed doors. Christopher Robinson's physical and emotional attacks on his partner were in stark contrast to the image portrayed in public. To the outsider, his marriage appeared "happy and devoted", a judge remarked at the bully's sentencing hearing, when the reality was "entirely different". At times, even the couple's distraught children were forced to watch, with Robinson taking their phones so they could not call for help, but Maidstone Crown Court heard that one youngster was eventually able to alert a relative and police were called. Upon arriving at the family home, officers were met by a belligerent Robinson, who tried to stop them from talking to his injured wife, but despite her husband's obstructiveness, she found the courage to reveal the ordeal she had suffered at his hands, which had escalated in seriousness during the 18 months prior to his arrest. On one occasion, the 36-year-old had strangled her with a phone charger cable until she blacked out, then, after the terrified woman regained consciousness, Robinson warned he would wait a minute before doing it again. Fearing she would die, the mum told her husband he would kill her, only for him to sinisterly reply: "No, I won't take it that far, I'll just torture you," and other episodes of abuse included repeated beatings, whipping her with tea towels and clothes, pouring drinks over her and forcing her to clean up food he had thrown on the floor. Robinson, of Invicta Road, Sheerness, later admitted offences of controlling or coercive behaviour, intentional strangulation and assault occasioning actual bodily harm. He was jailed for three years and four months and was also made subject to a 10-year restraining order. Kathleen Perry A deceitful mother and daughter who cruelly defrauded a 91-year-old woman with dementia out of more than £2,000 were jailed for a total of four years. Serial fraudster Kathleen Perry tricked her way into Queenie Watts' home in Maidstone and, having nabbed her bank card, went on a four-day spending spree with Siobhan Perry. The mean-spirited pair, who were both only recently out of jail and already had more than 200 crimes between them to their names, made multiple cashpoint withdrawals and frittered away their ill-gotten gains buying items including cigarettes, vapes, and trainers, as well as class A drugs. Perry Snr, who also goes by Kathlene, was not only on prison licence at the time of her fraud but also subject to a criminal behaviour order (CBO) banning her from such illicit activity. In total, the 61-year-old grandmother and her 43-year-old daughter, both from Maidstone and addicted to heroin and crack cocaine respectively, helped themselves to £2,303 of Miss Watts' money. Described as frail, confused and reliant on the help of daily carers, her family told Maidstone Crown Court it was "difficult to comprehend how anyone could be so heartless." Kathleen Perry, of Acorn Place, Park Wood, pleaded guilty to burglary, 15 counts of fraud by false representation, and a CBO breach. Siobhan Perry, of Station Road, in the County town, admitted eight offences of fraud by false representation. Kathleen Perry was jailed for a total of three years and three months, while Siobhan Perry was locked up for nine months, however, due to time served on remand since arrest, Perry Jnr was expected to be immediately released. Helen Tillcock A care worker who frenziedly stabbed her neighbour 11 times with a large kitchen knife in a "brewing conflict" over noise was jailed for six-and-a-half years. Helen Tillcock was drunk when she left Sarah Hodges with multiple wounds - five of which were life-threatening, and one considered fatal without treatment - to her head, face and upper body when she attacked her in the communal garden at the property they shared in Gillingham. The 43-year-old - said by her lawyer to have lived a life that was "miserable, difficult and full of hardship" but has no previous convictions - lashed out after Ms Hodges confronted her about banging doors in the early hours of July 20 last year. Tillcock, who was said to be "frustrated and irritated" by her next-door resident and had told her landlord just weeks earlier that she wanted to kill her and her cat, claimed she was the one who came under attack and had inflicted the injuries to Ms Hodges in self-defence. A jury at Maidstone Crown Court cleared her of attempted murder but she was found guilty of wounding Ms Hodges with intent to cause grievous bodily harm. The victim required two operations for stab and slash wounds to her scalp, temple, ear, nose, upper chest wall, left shoulder, arm and underarm, as well as defensive injuries. In the meantime, Tillcock fled to another neighbour - sneaking briefly back to her flat to grab a change of clothes - before being found by police hiding in a boiler cupboard in an unoccupied basement flat and arrested. Tillcock, who was also handed a 10-year restraining order, will be released after serving two-thirds of the jail term, less time spent on remand. Bilal Tahir A BMW driver who was driving at almost 100mph in wet conditions seconds before hitting and killing two women on a motorway hard shoulder was jailed for four years after the fatal crash. Dr Habiba Hajallie and Lisa Webber died in a horror crash on the M20 in Swanley in October 2022, which also left another man injured. Dr Hajallie, 35, aquaplaned on the coastbound carriageway near West Kingsdown and hit the barrier, damaging her vehicle. Ms Webber saw the incident and pulled over alongside the stricken vehicle, which was facing in the wrong direction, getting out of her car to help Dr Hajallie. But an inquest, which was opened and adjourned on October 28 that year, heard a third vehicle then left the carriageway and struck them and their cars with Dr Hajallie suffering blunt force trauma to her torso, while Ms Webber sustained multiple injuries. Both women died at the scene. A third person, Mark Heap, was injured in the incident after also pulling over to aid Dr Hajallie and the 55-year-old dad-of-three broke a bone in his back and one of his legs after he was pushed up against the crash barrier. Bilal Tahir, of South Gipsy Road, Welling, Bexleyheath, initially passed roadside tests for intoxication and impairment at the scene, before being released under investigation, but the 29-year-old was later charged with two counts of causing death by dangerous driving and one count of causing serious injury by dangerous driving. Officers from the Serious Collision Investigation Unit found, through vehicle and mobile phone data, he had continuously been speeding throughout his journey, hitting speeds of more than 100mph within a mile of the incident, and five seconds before the collision, Tahir had been travelling at 98mph when it is thought he lost control after hitting standing water. Tahir was at the wheel of a BMW X5 when the tragedy happened and pleaded guilty to the offences on March 23 this year, and earlier this month, he was sentenced to a total of 15 years' imprisonment at the Old Bailey earlier this month. Dane May A man who terrorised three members of his family, including a professional footballer, with threats of violence, rape and death was jailed for more than seven years. Dane May's disturbing and relentless behaviour against two siblings and their mother over 18 months culminated in a terrifying bid to attack one brother with a paving slab after smashing his way into his home. So alarming was the drug addict's vendetta against his relatives that his mum was forced to move house for her safety and his youngest brother - League One striker Alfie May - installed high-tech security at his home and worked out 'escape plans' with his wife. In one particularly sinister message sent to his oldest sibling, Sam May he warned: "I always said you would see me on the news and on the front page of every newspaper for what I would do to prove how f***ed up I really am." Details of how the lives of Sam, Alfie and their mum Deborah Rawlings were targeted by the 38-year-old before he erupted in violent rage on April 30 last year were revealed at his sentencing hearing for three offences of stalking from November 2023 and attempting to cause grievous bodily harm with intent in April last year. Maidstone Crown Court heard that after Dane inherited £120k from his late father at the end of 2023 and the family believed he used the money to fuel his drug addiction. It was also said there may have been "some dispute" over the division of assets from the estate, and although regarded as "a friendly, affable and good brother" when not abusing illegal substances and alcohol, while intoxicated he became "highly abusive and threatening," the court heard. May, of Edington Way, West Malling, initially denied the charges but pleaded guilty on what was the first day of his trial and earlier this month he was jailed for seven-and-a-half years - of which he must serve two-thirds before the parole board can consider his release - and a licence period extension of three years. Harry Homewood A sexual predator was jailed after assaulting two teenage girls who had been on a night out. Harry Homewood, 22, targeted his victims in the early hours of Saturday, March 14, this year as they walked in Station Road East in Canterbury. Homewood, formerly of Augustine Road, Ramsgate, approached the duo at about 2.30am, touching them under and over their clothing while making inappropriate remarks. Despite efforts to stop him, he carried out repeated sexual assaults before his victims were able to alert friends in the area, who subsequently told police. Homewood was arrested shortly after and was charged the following day. After pleading guilty to multiple sexual offences, Homewood was sentenced at Canterbury Crown Court on June 4 to three years and nine months' imprisonment. He will be subject to police notification requirements indefinitely. Investigating officer DC Kirsty Chilcott said: "Every woman and girl has the right to feel safe while they are out socialising and the repulsive, aggressive behaviour of sexual predators such as Homewood will never be tolerated." Luke Wells A banned driver who left two people seriously injured and one close to death when he ploughed into their car at almost twice the speed limit was jailed. Luke Wells was at the wheel of a Vauxhall Astra when he struck Terrance Brown's Peugeot at 58mph on Queenborough Road in Halfway, Sheerness, on July 20, 2024. In Mr Brown's car were his wife, Susan, their granddaughter, Chelsea Brown and her boyfriend Jack Fitzgibbon, but instead of stopping to help the injured parties, Wells fled the scene, kicking off his sliders footwear as he ran away and once home, at nearby Lapwing Close in Minster, he showered and shaved off his hair. Then, when traced by police, he repeatedly lied, telling them he had previously sold the Astra and had only been out on his bicycle that day. His account soon began to unravel, and his fate was sealed when one of his children mentioned he had been driving and "speeding", Maidstone Crown Court heard. In the meantime, those he had abandoned at the scene of the smash outside Sheerness East Working Men's Club had been taken to hospital and Mrs Brown, the front seat passenger, had to be cut from the wreckage and airlifted to King's College Hospital in London, where she spent four weeks in an induced coma. Her injuries included fractures to her collarbone, 12 ribs, breastbone, pelvis and left forearm, and she also suffered bruising and a partial lung collapse. Doctors were so concerned that at one stage they told the family she might not survive. Wells, now 31 but 29 at the time, pleaded guilty to three offences of causing serious injury by dangerous driving and one of perverting the course of justice. At his sentencing hearing earlier this month, Wells, who has eight convictions for 25 offences, was jailed for three years and 10 months and was also given a five-year, 11-month driving ban, after which he will be required to pass an extended test to obtain a licence. Mohammad Tajik and Alnour Ali A small boat pilot who asked passengers to check YouTube for guidance during a Channel crossing was locked up. A court heard Afghan national Mohammad Tajik, 32, was steering an overcrowded dinghy when he asked others on board to search the video-sharing site for assistance. He was jailed alongside another migrant pilot in the first sentencing under a new law targeting dangerous small boat crossings and Tajik was sentenced to two years in prison at Canterbury Crown Court over the dangerous journey on January 17. At the same time, Sudanese national Alnour Ali, 26, was sentenced to 27 months for a crossing on April 9. Judge Simon James said: "The inherent dangers of seeking to navigate one of the busiest shipping lanes in the world in a vessel which was never designed to undertake such a journey are obvious." Endangering others during a journey by sea to the UK is an offence that came into force in January as part of new border security legislation, and Tajik was the first to be convicted under the new law, having pleaded guilty at the same court on April 21. Of Tajik's crossing, footage was shown to the court of the boat, overcrowded with people sitting around the edge and for Ali, drone footage was played in court of the boat with 74 people on board, where Ali was seen driving while wearing a pink hat. Passengers were seen straddling the side of the boat, with some legs dangling in the water and a French boat comes up alongside the dinghy and hands out life-jackets. The pair both have submitted claims for asylum since being in the UK, the court also heard. Tallanius Bridgland Police body-cam footage showed the moment a drug dealer was found hiding in his kitchen and trying to destroy his mobile phone. The video captures officers storming the property in Freeman's Way, Deal, and apprehending Tallanius Bridgland. Investigators had gathered evidence suggesting significant drug-dealing activity was taking place at the premises, and the 31-year-old was found to be in possession of 22 individual wraps of cocaine. He was arrested on suspicion of possession with intent to supply a Class A drug. He was cautioned, he tried to convince officers it was for 'personal use' and further searches of the property led to the discovery of 93 packets of cannabis edibles. Several bank cards which did not belong to Bridgland were also found and after examining them, suspicious financial activity was identified involving large sums of money, totalling approximately £50,000, moving in and out of the account in quick succession. Bridgland's phone was later found to contain images and videos of drugs, including footage showing multiple bags of cannabis edibles, similar to those seized at the property. Following the raid in March 2021, he was charged with possession with intent to supply Class A and Class B drugs and concealing criminal property. He later pleaded guilty to the offences. He was sentenced to two years' imprisonment at Canterbury Crown Court earlier this month. Harvey Doughty A paedophile previously spared jail for abusing a young boy was placed behind bars after his phone revealed indecent images of children and sexualised exchanges with a chatbot. The discovery was made following an unannounced visit to Harvey Doughty's home by police officers responsible for managing him in the community. Just 12 months earlier, he had been handed a suspended sentence order (SSO) and made subject to sex offender notification requirements after pleading guilty to sexual activity with a child and causing or inciting a child to engage in sexual activity. Maidstone Crown Court heard on that occasion that he had been "exploring his sexuality" when, as an 18-year-old, he molested his vulnerable victim, and it was said that Doughty, of Knott Court, Maidstone, was "ashamed and sorry" for his conduct, committed when he felt "confused and inexperienced". Furthermore, his lawyer argued that when it came to deciding the appropriate sentence, "a great deal of prominence" could be given to rehabilitation when balancing it with the need for public protection. In turn, Doughty was told that an 18-month jail term could be suspended for two years, but the same court - albeit a different judge - heard on June 10 that when officers called at his home on November 3 last year to carry out a compliance check, they became concerned about the amount of data and online conversations taking place on his phone. Prosecutor Nathan Fitzpatrick said the device was seized and, when analysed, found to contain nine child abuse images, two of which were in the most serious categorisation and depicted penetrative sexual activity and also found on the device were a number of accounts that the police had not been notified of including one for Discord, one for Snapchat and one for the YouTube web browsing service. Now 23, he admitted three offences of making indecent images of children in respect of their downloading, failing to comply with notification requirements, and breach of an SSO by the commission of further offending and Doughty was jailed for 10 months. He will be required to sign on the sex offender register for 10 years, and be subject to a sexual harm prevention order (SHPO) in relation to his use of the internet for the same period. Connor Appleton A thug who struck a woman over the head with a metal bar before using his car as a weapon to hit a man was locked up. Connor Appleton attacked the pair during a violent early-hours confrontation which left both victims needing hospital treatment. The incident happened in Spital Street, Dartford, at about 3.30am on November 25, 2023 and the 27-year-old had been driving when he became involved in an argument with a group of women walking past. As the dispute escalated, he got out of his car, carrying a metal bar and approached the group. He struck one of the women over the head, causing her to fall to the ground. When members of the public tried to intervene, Appleton swung the metal bar towards them before he returned to his car and drove aggressively, turning towards and striking a man on foot. Shocking footage of the incident showed Appleton, of Wall Close, Rochester, wielding the metal bar and driving off from the incident. Bodycam footage from police later showed him being arrested at his home at 5.30am the same morning. He pleaded guilty to dangerous driving, affray, attempted grievous bodily harm with intent, possession of an offensive weapon and inflicting grievous bodily harm, and he was jailed for five years and one month at Woolwich Crown Court earlier this month. William Hutchings Footage showed the moment a dangerous driver crashed head-on with another vehicle while being chased by the police. William Hutchings was travelling along London Road in Sevenoaks towards Amherst Hill in a Ford Fiesta when police saw him performing a dangerous overtaking manoeuvre. But after the police indicated he needed to stop, the 31-year-old continued to travel at excessive speed and a pursuit ensued. Video showed him whizzing down roads, travelling at high speed on hatched markings and on the wrong side of the road. Once on the A25 Westerham Road, near Sevenoaks, the offender collided head-on with a black Land Rover Defender. Investigating officer DC Vikki Steward said it was "fortunate" the occupants were not seriously injured or killed, as they suffered minor injuries. Hutchings, formerly of London, suffered life-threatening injuries which required officers administering life-saving first aid until paramedics arrived, and the footage also showed the aftermath with remnants of the silver Ford Fiesta obliterated across the road. The incident, which took place on April 19, 2025, resulted in Hutchings having a lengthy stay in hospital and after his discharge, he was interviewed and charged with dangerous driving. He pleaded guilty and was sentenced to 16 months in prison at Maidstone Crown June 4. Following his release, Hutchings will be made the subject of a driving ban for five years and six months and will be required to take an extended test after this period. Cameron Langley-McColm The son of a convicted murderer was driving at an "eye-watering" 80mph in a 30mph zone when he ploughed into and killed a teenager. Kieran Byrne-Nicholls was making his way home along Heath Road in Coxheath when he was one of two people struck by a white 1 Series BMW M Sport on July 8, 2023. Cameron Langley-McColm, of Coombe Road, Maidstone, was charged with causing death by dangerous driving in January and pleaded guilty at the first opportunity. At his sentencing hearing at Maidstone Crown Court, it was said he was picked up on CCTV overtaking another vehicle at speed moments before the crash. The court was told that Kieran, who was on an e-bike, and another boy were in the wrong lane at the time, but they knew they could move onto the pavement to let a car pass, but as the BMW overtook them, the other boy was clipped first, causing him to fall and hit his head before Kieran was struck from behind, causing him to be "projected" into the air and tragically died at the scene. The then 20-year-old had reached speeds of 82mph in what is a 30mph zone and a narrow, single-carriageway B-road with traffic calming measures and it was said Langley-McColm was "rushing to pick up his mother" - who was jailed in May 2024 for stabbing Maidstone pub landlord Matthew Bryant - when he struck the pair at around 10pm. Judge Oliver Saxby KC said that Langley-McColm's speed was "eye-watering", and the court heard that the now 23-year-old had four other people in his car at the time, but none of them mentioned his "excessive speed" in their police statements. He jailed the young dad for seven years and six months, of which he will serve two-thirds before being released on licence, and he was also disqualified from driving for six years upon his release. To read more of our in-depth coverage of all of the major trials coming out of crown and magistrates' courts across the county, click here. To see who was locked up in May, click here. Jaskirat Singh A man who earned £185,000 smuggling people from the UK to France in the back of lorries was put behind bars. Jaskirat Singh, 25, was accused of arranging clandestine travel for non-British nationals between December 2024 and March 2026. The plot was exposed when 11 Indian nationals were found hiding in the trailer of a lorry stopped at Dover and the information they provided, along with the discovery of four other Indian nationals in a separate lorry travelling to France in January 2025, led authorities to Singh and his address in Wolverhampton. The Immigration Enforcement-led investigation found a wealth of incriminating evidence when they examined Singh's mobile devices, which included voice notes, images, messages and videos, some of which were saved from TikTok advertising crossings to France. The prosecution said this was evidence Singh was interested and involved in smuggling people across the English Channel and a video of a large number of £20 notes spread out on a bed was likely to have been filmed by Singh. Another showed someone else showing migrants getting off a lorry in an unknown location, with the person filming saying, "UK to France", suggesting that it was a successful smuggling incident. Singh also bragged in a voice message about successfully arranging for up to 60 people to be driven to France every week over nine months, but it is not clear if he was exaggerating to get more business. One string of messages found on his phone from June 2025 was to a Romanian mobile discussing payment for 15 people who had been smuggled to France in a lorry, and the chat confirmed the driver was paid £5,500 for the human cargo. After pleading guilty to one count of conspiracy to assist unlawful immigration to a member state, he was sentenced to five years and three months in jail at Canterbury Crown Court earlier this month. Singh will return to court at a later date, where the CPS will seek to recover the money he made from his crime to ensure he cannot benefit from it in the future. Andrew Sharp A twisted rapist who hoarded hundreds of images of his victim among thousands of other depraved pictures was jailed for more than 13 years. Andrew Sharp, of Station Road in Lydd, first came to police attention last year after he uploaded an indecent image of a child online. This illegal online activity was flagged to the police, who launched a full investigation and obtained a warrant to search his property in June 2025. The 69-year-old was not in the country at the time officers executed the warrant, but was notified of the seizure of his digital assets. Digital forensics experts analysed a laptop and uncovered a cache of child sexual abuse images, and upon re-entering the country, Sharp sent a message to a contact on his phone where he confessed to possession of illicit pictures and revealed that he had also taken indecent images of a young girl. He was arrested upon his return on June 21 and interviewed in custody, and the sex offender admitted possessing indecent images found on his laptop, and revealed that he had sexually abused a young girl. He was charged with sexual assault and two other indecent image-related offences and as the investigation progressed, while Sharp was held on remand, further laptops were discovered, and across his devices, detectives found 17,382 child abuse images, including hundreds depicting his young victim, which led detectives to believe she had been raped. A total of 1,300 pictures were Category A - the most extreme type and in November 2025, he pleaded guilty to 12 additional charges, including rape. Earlier this month, at Canterbury Crown Court, Sharp was sentenced to 13 years and six months in prison, and the court also granted a restraining order and a Sexual Harm Prevention Order (SHPO) for 20 years. Andrew Veck A teenage girl secretly filmed in the shower by her paedophile brother-in-law has branded him "evil" after it emerged she was not his first victim. Andrew Veck narrowly avoided prison last year after he was convicted of recording Hollie Boyce in the bathroom of her sister's home in Ramsgate when she was just 14. She spoke out again after police searching devices seized from Veck found footage showing another woman being filmed using the toilet years earlier, revealing he had struck before. The discovery came after the 34-year-old was hauled back before the courts for repeatedly breaching restrictions imposed following his original conviction. Speaking after he was finally jailed, Hollie said she was "not surprised in the slightest" and added: "Someone this evil isn't fixable, ever." Veck was sentenced in February last year after admitting voyeurism, making an indecent image of a child and three counts of possessing indecent images, including 21 depicting the most serious forms of abuse and he received a 16-month prison sentence suspended for two years. He was made subject to a Sexual Harm Prevention Order (SHPO) for 10 years. However, he appeared at Canterbury Crown Court this month after admitting six breaches of the order, as well as a further act of voyeurism, driving without a licence or insurance and possession of cannabis after police carried out an unannounced visit to his address in August last year, after concerns he was breaching the terms designed to manage the risk he posed to women and children. Officers found a number of internet-enabled devices that had not been declared to police, including a mobile phone, Xbox, hard drives, memory cards, an MP3 player and USB sticks and officers also found an iPhone hidden in a loft after they heard it ringing. A small amount of cannabis was also found. Veck was sentenced to 28 months in prison for the SHPO breaches, to be served in addition to 12 months for breaching his suspended sentence and a 17-week sentence for the historic voyeurism charge will run concurrently, while he was also fined £50 for cannabis possession and given six points on his licence for the driving offence. Christopher Hutson A convicted child rapist who was due to become eligible for parole next year is staying behind bars after being sentenced for horrific abuse against two more victims. A judge told Christopher Hutson he had shown no insight into this "cynical, manipulative and exploitative" criminality and therefore continued to pose a significant risk of serious harm to the public. The former resident of Lodge Oak Lane, Tonbridge, was originally locked up for 14 years in November 2018 for a series of heinous sex crimes which dated back to 2014, and included filming himself while raping a child. Having been told he would have to serve two-thirds of that jail term before he could apply for parole, his earliest release date was expected to be November 11, 2027, but in 2023, when the paedophile was already in a category D prison and working towards being allowed temporary release, police were informed of similar deviant offending committed in 2011 against two other youngsters. It came to light after one victim detailed some of what they had suffered at Hutson's hands in a letter to a parent. Hutson was subsequently charged with seven offences, including rape, engaging in sexual activity in the presence of a child, and causing or inciting a child to engage in sexual activity. Having denied the allegations - claiming the victims were lying - he stood trial in March this year at Maidstone Crown Court and was found guilty by the jury and at his sentencing hearing, the 66-year-old heard how the ordeals the children suffered at his hands had had a profound and long-lasting impact. Hutson, whose previous 14-year jail term was part of an 18-year extended sentence, was handed a 10-year extended sentence comprising eight years' imprisonment and a two-year additional licence period. He was told the custodial element, of which he must serve two-thirds, will not begin until the current one expires in July 2032. Hutson will also be subject to sex offender notification requirements, as well as a sexual harm prevention order, for life. Roland Simmons A paedophile teacher who abused young boys at a children's home - including targeting one victim as he slept - was jailed for 14 years. Roland Simmons, from Chartham, near Canterbury, carried out the offences while working at Guestling House, near Hastings, in the 1970s. Four victims, now all in their 60s, came forward decades later to report the abuse they suffered while in care at the property and following a trial at Lewes Crown Court in April, the 77-year-old was convicted of 10 counts of indecent assault against four boys between 1974 and 1979. He returned to the same court this month, where he was sentenced to 14 years in prison. Speaking at the trial, Catherine Wear, senior crown prosecutor from the CPS, said: "Simmons was supposed to be caring for these boys, but instead he abused the trust placed in him, knowing that the boys would struggle to speak out and report what was happening to them. "One victim woke to find Simmons abusing him. Simmons then claimed that he had been dreaming and everything was fine. "Another victim described Simmons as "persistent" after he first abused him, saying that Simmons would ask him to go out in his car alone, but he kept refusing. "The trauma of sexual abuse can stop victims from making complaints for many years, but these men had the courage to come forward and report what happened to them and by doing so, have ensured that Simmons has been brought to justice for his appalling crimes." Luke Carney A drug dealer who stored firearms in his van was locked up. Police attended the home of Luke Carney in Alder Way, Swanley, to execute a warrant and, although he was absent during a search, weapons were seized from a van registered in his name. A day after, on November 7, 2025, the 28-year-old was detained on the M4 by a patrol from South Wales Police. He was later charged with possession of a prohibited weapon - a revolver, possession of a firearm without a certificate and possession of a shotgun without a certificate. Carney was released on conditional court bail and on December 11, a further search warrant was executed at his home address. Approximately £12,000 in cash and counterfeit money, drug paraphernalia and four mobile phones were seized. He was arrested and later charged with possession of criminal property and being concerned in the supply of Class A drugs. After admitting all the offences, he was handed a five-year jail term at Woolwich Crown Court on Monday, June 8. Alan Richens A sex offender was jailed for sharing what the lead investigator described as the worst images and videos she had ever seen. Alan Richens, from Maidstone, was sentenced to three years' imprisonment for distributing hundreds of indecent images of very young children on the Telegram app. He will also be subject to a 15-year Sexual Harm Prevention Order (SHPO) following his release, and he will remain on the sex offenders' register for life. On November 12 last year, police were made aware that indecent images of children had been uploaded to the internet and Richens was identified as the perpetrator. Officers forced entry to his home address in London Road, where they found him sleeping and he was arrested. His mobile phone was seized, and forensic analysts reviewed the content on the device and found obscene Telegram messages between Richens and another account, in which they discussed sexual abuse of children. Throughout the conversation, the 51-year-old shared more than 218 indecent images. Investigators later discovered conversation threads of a similar theme with other Telegram users and Richens was charged with making indecent photographs of children and distributing indecent photographs of children. He pleaded guilty to the offences and was sentenced at Maidstone Crown Court on Monday, June 22. Lewis Densley A paedophile who failed to comply with a sexual risk order was jailed after failing to notify police he was staying at a woman's house with her kids present. Lewis Densley from Northfleet was supposed to tell officers about any developing relationships with women and was not allowed to have unsupervised contact with children after being convicted of sex offences. He was made subject to this sexual risk order in February 2025, after being convicted of engaging in sexual communication with a child. The order also meant he could not use any computer or device capable of accessing the internet unless he notified the police in advance, but in December, the police received a report that the 30-year-old had been sending sexual messages to two women. Officers carried out further checks and found he had a hidden phone with images and messages sent to an individual in May 2025. When arrested Densley, of Beaumont Drive, had two phones on him, and it was discovered that he had also begun a relationship with another woman and had stayed at her house when her children were present, failing to notify police of this. He was charged with six counts of breaching a sexual risk order. Following guilty pleas, he was jailed for two years and nine months on Monday, June 22 at Woolwich Crown Court. Tobias Dono A violent bully, said to have "hyper masculine beliefs", who repeatedly punched his ex-partner in the head before she fell from an open door of a car travelling at 40mph was jailed. Cocaine addict Tobias Dono's frightening assault on the woman occurred at the end of a relationship marred by his drug-taking, abuse, threats, aggression and control. Maidstone Crown Court heard that during their 17 months together, the 33-year-old isolated her from friends, limited her life to being at work or in his company, stopped her sunbed sessions, monitored her phone, and smashed up their flat in angry rages. As well as telling her he did not want her "looking good" and asking who she was "trying to impress", he insulted her using derogatory terms such as "retarded c**t" and "s**t", and while holidaying in Spain, he spent their combined money meant for food purchases on cocaine. On a subsequent visit to Amsterdam, he went to buy drugs, leaving her alone and taking her passport and having moved in together in late 2024, the couple then went on a trip to Thailand, the court was told. But his abuse escalated and, after again abandoning her, he returned, banging on the room door before ripping out a window to gain access, and once inside, he dragged her from the bed, tried to have her ejected by security, and threw her clothes in the pool. Although her mum paid for her to have a separate room, he hounded her relatives to find out where she was and despite her best efforts to avoid him, he shouted at her so much during part of the journey home that a fellow passenger handed her a leaflet on domestic abuse in response to her distress. Dono, who has previous convictions for battery, possessing offensive weapons, criminal damage and stalking, later admitted controlling or coercive behaviour, assault occasioning actual bodily harm, putting a person in fear of violence by harassment, and two offences of perverting the course of justice. Dono, of no fixed address, was given an eight-year and four-month extended prison sentence which consists of a five-year, four-month jail term - of which he must serve at least two-thirds - and an additional three-year licence period and was also given a 15-year restraining order in respect of his victim. Azzeddine Ainbouzid An Algerian man caught on drone footage piloting a small boat with more than 60 people across the English Channel was jailed for two years. Azzeddine Ainbouzid was sentenced earlier this month Canterbury Crown Court for endangering the lives of others. There were 66 other people on board the boat, including a pregnant woman and three children and the vehicle was designed to carry 20 passengers. The prosecution said that when a boat is so overloaded, then its speed, manoeuvrability and seaworthiness are severely restricted. Some passengers were not wearing a lifejacket, and it was so overcrowded that people had to sit along the sides with their feet in the water. When a Border Force rescue vessel came close, 29-year-old Ainbouzid was seen taking his hand off the tiller. This caused the boat to lose control and start going round in circles, further putting those on board in danger, and Ainbouzid was identified as the pilot by the logo on the left-hand sleeve of his black jacket and his distinctive facial hair and was charged by the Crown Prosecution Service. The investigation by Immigration Enforcement revealed that this was the second time he had entered the UK illegally, having previously arrived in 2023 and later that year, he was convicted for being drunk and disorderly and resisting or obstructing a constable and was given a conditional discharge for 12 months. He also had convictions in France for drug trafficking and aggravated theft and the CPS asked the court to take these incidents into account when deciding what sentence to hand down. Ainbouzid is the third person to be sent to prison for endangering the lives of others.
